mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations and reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate indicates deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth is below 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail sales and accelerating inflation indicate overheated consumer demand that forces aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth is below 5% annually, or interaction term Rank IC is negative over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290, market participants price in imminent aggressive rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates confirm robust economic expansion without overheating, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 300k but UNRATE simultaneously rises, or interaction Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the signal shows zero predictive power after adjusting for market valuation levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and tightening credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS is below 2%, or the signal produces positive returns in >65% of triggered months.
